resources.string: A virtual machine manager powered by Qualcomm Gunyah Hypervisor. Create and manage lightweight Linux VMs directly on your device.

Gunyah is a Type-1 hypervisor developed by Qualcomm that runs directly on the hardware beneath the Android host OS. It allows creating isolated virtual machines with near-native performance and strong security guarantees, without relying on kernel-level virtualization (KVM).

Currently only devices with Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Elite (SM8750) or newer SoCs that ship with Gunyah support enabled are supported. Older Snapdragon chipsets do not include the necessary hypervisor firmware.

1. Root access (e.g. Magisk or KernelSU).\n2. The virtualization APEX (com.android.virt) must be installed, which provides the crosvm binary.\n3. A supported Qualcomm SoC with Gunyah hypervisor enabled in firmware.
This check did not pass. Skipping may cause unexpected issues. Are you sure you want to continue?
\u274C Root access denied. Please grant root permission and retry.
\u274C Virtualization support not found. Please ensure the com.android.virt APEX is available.
\u274C All files access is required to manage VM disk images. Tap to grant permission.
\u2705 Prebuilt files are up to date.
\u274C Failed to extract prebuilt files. Please retry.
All checks and prepares passed.\nYou are ready to use DroidVM.
Tap the + button to create one.
Are you sure you want to delete this virtual machine?
The virtual machine exited with code %1$d.\n\nLogs:
A VM with this name already exists
Selected hypervisor is not supported by selected backend
Untagged-only (0) ports are not supported on a Linux bridge; use a tagged VLAN or trunk
VLAN must be 0-4094, or 4095 for trunk
One per line: [proto] host:guest (no proto = tcp+udp), ranges like 8000-9000:8000-9000
Port forwards only take effect with SNAT enabled
Enable DHCPv%1$s on network \"%2$s\" VLAN %3$d first

Custom firmware only takes effect on the QEMU backend\ncrosvm always uses the built-in EDK2\nBooting Linux via UEFI is still being adapted and is not usable yet -- to boot Linux, choose the Linux kernel boot protocol
No disk image configured for this VM
Must be a number of seconds
Pinned entry "%1$s" not found; using default

This kernel lacks CONFIG_DMA_RESTRICTED_POOL.\nUnder a protected VM the guest cannot share DMA memory with the host, so virtio devices (disk/network) will fail to start.\nUse the built-in kernel, or rebuild with CONFIG_DMA_RESTRICTED_POOL.
Analyze the image\'s kernel/initrd over the network before importing
Enter a valid http(s):// image URL first
Backup VM, networks and disk configs to a JSON file
Restore VM, networks and disks configs from a JSON file
This will overwrite your existing VM, disk, and network configurations with the data from the selected file. Are you sure you want to continue?
The selected file is not a valid configuration backup.
Automatically open the console when a VM is started
Clear all console logs for the VM before it starts
Automatically check for updates when the app starts
You are using the latest version
API information not loaded. Please agree to the privacy policy first.
crosvm can\'t read compressed qcow2 images -- the VM would boot to disk I/O errors with no partition table. Convert it to uncompressed and start?\n%1$s
Are you sure you want to remove this disk entry?
You are trying to optimize an incremental image. Please choose an action.\nKeep incremental image and optimize only\nConvert to a standalone image
A disk with this name already exists
This image is not managed by DroidVM
Apply snapshot \"%s\"? This will overwrite the current disk state.
Write zeros to fill the entire file now
Backing image cannot be the same as the target disk
Please enter a valid mirror URL.
Enter a URL and tap Load to fetch info
The disk operation is still in progress. Are you sure you want to cancel?
Also change password for normal users
The operation is still in progress. Cancelling will stop and delete the temporary VM. Are you sure?
\nOperation failed. Please check the logs above.\n
The new size is smaller than the current size. Shrinking the disk may cause data loss. Are you sure you want to continue?
Target format is the same as current
Are you sure you want to cancel downloading \"%1$s\"?
A download is already in progress. Please wait for it to finish.
Download continues in the background. Check the notification shade.
Could not start the download. Make sure the system Download Manager is enabled.
An outdated daemon is running. Would you like to restart it?\n\nNote: All running VMs will be stopped.

You are running a test build of DroidVM.

This version is for testing purposes only and may contain bugs or incomplete features. Data loss or instability may occur.

Please report any issues on GitHub.
Are you sure you want to delete this network?
Cannot delete: network is in use by running VM: %1$s
A network with this name already exists
Must start with a letter and contain only letters, digits, hyphens, or underscores
A network with this bridge name already exists
This network is running. Stop it before editing.
Wi-Fi uplinks cannot be bridged directly; the pseudo-bridge is required.
This uplink can be bridged directly; the pseudo-bridge is optional.
VM-internal network: the host does not participate. Add a VLAN for the host to join.
Network is active and forwarding traffic
Failed to connect to VNC server
No USB keyboard or mouse found
No app found to handle VNC connections
About to open with an external app
The selected display \"%1$s\" is currently %2$s. Casting may not work properly.\n\nDo you want to continue anyway?
Slide to move cursor\nTap to click\nTwo-finger scroll
Please read and agree to the privacy policy before continuing
If you decline the privacy policy, some features may not be available. Are you sure you want to decline?
Are you sure you want to go back? All changes will be lost.
The gh-hugepage-reserve Magisk module is not installed. Please download and install it first.
The hugepage reserve module crashed during last boot.
Are you sure you want to unload the hugepage reserve module? This will release all pre-allocated pages and may affect running VMs.
DroidVM downloads disk images in the background and shows the progress in a notification. Allow notifications so you can keep an eye on the download.
Gunyah Resource Manager refuses to start the virtual machine
There are some issues with the kernel running on your device. Try replacing or recompiling it.\nTry enabling or disabling the "Prepare Lend mTHP" option.\nOpen the browser for more information.
Virtual machine %s ran into insufficient huge page memory.\nTry changing the memory size, or use the pre-allocation module.\nOpen the browser for more information.
The kernel of virtual machine %s does not support Restricted DMA Pool.\nPlease replace the kernel or use the built-in DroidVM kernel.\nOpen the browser for more information.
The Gunyah version on your device is too old (for example, Snapdragon 8 Gen 2). Try updating the system or switching to a newer device.
crosvm\'s sandbox is not available on this platform. Please disable sandbox.
